title: Range Rover answers the question: "What if we built a not-SUV?"
author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
body: Range Rover provided flights from Washington, DC, to London and accommodation so Ars could see the RR GT, as well as some other things you can read about in early September. Ars does not accept paid editorial content. WOLVERHAMPTON, England—One can argue about whether or not Range Rover invented the luxury SUV category, but there's no question that since the first Range Rover went on sale in 1970, the brand has made itself the standard that other luxury SUVs have to measure against. But what if Range Rover built something that wasn't an SUV? That's exactly what it's doing with the new Range Rover GT. Spy shots have been circulating for at least a year of what was believed to be an all-electric replacement for the Range Rover Velar . It turns out that was a misapprehension; the heavily camouflaged prototypes were examples of the Range Rover GT being tested at the Nürburgring and the Arctic Circle . Will this be Range Rover's answer to the Lucid Air and Porsche Taycan? Credit: Range Rover Range Rover still isn't ready to reveal an uncamouflaged GT, but the car you see is much less disguised than the boxy-looking prototypes seen in earlier spy shots. Read full article Comments

title: Trump administration to spend $1 billion on new border surveillance system
body: More than a thousand new surveillance towers will be erected along U.S. borders, according to a recent Department of Homeland Security (DHS) assessment by the Government Accounting Office. The expanded autonomous surveillance plan , including tech upgrades to existing towers overseen by DHS, is estimated to cost around $1 billion and aims to be finished by 2034. Upon completion, the country's borders will boast 2,300 surveillance towers it currently operates around 830, the Electronic Frontier Foundation reports. SEE ALSO: Google's AI Search is a minefield for schools The expenditure is part of the Customs and Border Protection's (CBP) consolidated Integrated Surveillance Tower (IST) program, which operates a network of intel-gathering towers and surveillance that detect "items of interest" along the border and in nearby towns. The towers are integrated into a central system accessible by agents. "It s not just a barrier. A lot of people miss that [it] has technology built into it," said CBP Commissioner Rodney Scott during an April budget hearing with lawmakers regarding the agency's plans. Building on years of work to outfit border regions with technologically-advanced security systems, the White House has proposed millions in infrastructure for a "smart" border wall , including autonomous and AI-powered surveillance towers that alert agents to potential security threats. In 2025 and 2026, more than $240 million was allocated for the IST program. The 2027 budget steered another $97 million toward the construction of 95 new towers over the next year.

body: On Monday, District 9 and Gran Turismo director Neill Blomkamp unveiled his latest project: a 13-minute sci-fi short titled Nightborne that's loosely based on Peter Watts' 2014 novel Echopraxia . The short comes from Blomkamp's new AI startup / production company, Barley Studios, and features characters whose voices and faces are modeled after human actors. But every single one of Nightborne 's shots was made with ByteDance's Seedance 2.0 text-to-video generator . title: On the run for 20 years, most-wanted fugitive caught hiding as a biotech exec
author: Beth Mole
body: One of Rhode Island's most-wanted fugitives was arrested in New York last week after being on the run for more than 20 years, which was at least partially spent hiding as a biotech executive, according to Stat News . In a Securities and Exchange Commission filing Monday, California-based Immix Biopharma noted that Richard Graydon was terminated Friday for " reasons unrelated to his activities at the Company ." It turns out, Richard Graydon was an alias for Ronald Fischer, a 70-year-old former anesthesiologist licensed in Rhode Island and Massachusetts, who fled amid his 2005 trial for first-degree sexual assault. Fischer was accused of raping a woman during a date on his yacht, Lion King . Read full article Comments

title: Spotify launches its first global music video chart
body: Spotify is giving music videos their own Top 50. On Tuesday, July 21, Spotify launched its first global chart dedicated to music videos, ranking the 50 most-watched videos on the platform each day. Artists can also pitch new videos directly to Spotify's editorial team for the first time, giving them a chance to appear on playlists including Best New Videos, Live Performances, and Today's Top Videos. The features arrive after a steady expansion of music videos across Spotify, including full-length uploads, personalized recommendations, and early premieres from artists like BTS , Jelly Roll, and Taylor Swift . Artists can also now upload their own videos through Spotify for Artists. SEE ALSO: Spotify adds editor videos to New Music Friday. We asked its curators why. Add it all together, and Spotify is beginning to resemble a version of MTV built for the streaming era, complete with exclusive premieres, countdowns, and ever-changing rankings. There may not be a VJ telling viewers what comes next, but there is an AI DJ if you're into that sort of thing. Otherwise, Spotify's editors and recommendation algorithms fill the role. Spotify has built its own music video countdown The new Music Video Charts Global ranks videos by daily stream counts, refreshes daily at 6 p.m. ET, and lives in Spotify's Charts and Music Video Playlists hubs. Every Tuesday, the company will also highlight the week s top 10 videos across its social media accounts. Shakira and Burna Boy claimed the chart s inaugural No. 1 with "Dai Dai," the official song of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, which just surpassed 13

title: OpenAI says it accidentally hacked Hugging Face with a new AI system
body: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. | Bloomberg via Getty Images OpenAI says its AI models mistakenly breached open-source AI platform Hugging Face during internal testing. In a blog post on Tuesday , OpenAI writes that GPT-5.6 Sol and "an even more capable pre-release model" discovered vulnerabilities within their sandboxed testing environment, allowing them to gain access to the internet and target Hugging Face. On July 16th, Hugging Face disclosed a security incident that it says was driven by "an autonomous AI agent system." Hugging Face's AI agents detected and stopped the breach, which OpenAI has now admitted occurred during an evaluation of its models' cybersecurity capabilities. title: Judge approves Anthropics $1.5 billion settlement with authors
body: A federal judge in San Francisco has granted final approval to a $1.5 billion settlement between Anthropic and a class of authors who accused the AI company of illegally downloading their copyrighted books, closing out what the court called the largest copyright class action settlement in history. U.S. District Judge Araceli Mart nez-Olgu n signed the order on July 20 , resolving claims brought by lead plaintiffs Andrea Bartz, Kirk Wallace Johnson, and others over Anthropic's use of pirated libraries LibGen and PiLiMi to build its book collection. Notably, the underlying dispute centered on how Anthropic acquired the books, not on whether training AI models with copyrighted material is lawful. SEE ALSO: Anthropic to provide California government with AI tools In an earlier ruling cited throughout the order , the court had already determined that the use of training itself constituted fair use. Per the settlement, authors and publishers whose pirated books ended up on Anthropic's "Works List" can claim roughly $3,000 per book about four times the usual minimum for copyright infringement cases. More than 91 percent of eligible works, over 440,000 books, have already been claimed. Anthropic also has to delete the pirated files it downloaded. The settlement only releases Anthropic from liability for how it acquired the training data in the past not from future lawsuits over what the chatbot generates, or new claims going forward. Judge Araceli Mart nez-Olgu n was explicit that the settlement does not release claims for future harm or claims "based on the output of AI models." T

title: The NWSL is finding its next generation of fans online
body: The National Women's Soccer League (NWSL) is finding new fans in more places than the stadium. According to new data from the league , views across its YouTube channels increased 41.7 percent through the first 10 weeks of the 2026 season, reaching 11.2 million. Watch time climbed nearly 50 percent, while subscribers grew 17.9 percent. Those gains are part of a strong first half of the season for the NWSL . Attendance is up, the league s social following has expanded, and merchandise sales have increased. New clubs and a wider mix of broadcast and streaming options are also creating more ways for people to follow the league. So, who are these new fans, how are they discovering the NWSL, and what is turning their interest into a lasting connection with the league? Who is watching the NWSL? The NWSL's audience is broader than the young, predominantly female fan base often associated with women's sports. Nielsen estimated that the league had 39.3 million fans in the United States in 2025, up from 30.8 million in 2023. On ESPN , women accounted for nearly half of the NWSL audience for the second consecutive season, while viewership among women ages 18 to 34 increased 30 percent year over year. The league s average audience on ESPN Deportes also more than doubled from 2024, rising 109 percent. Women now account for nearly half of the league's audience on ESPN, while women ages 18 to 34 are among its fastest-growing viewer groups. Spanish-language viewership is also rising, introducing the league to more fans across the United States. That growing audience is increasingly visible

But that transformation might also be the only thing that can stop a shocking new threat to the city and those he loves–a powerful villain no one can even see. title: Confusion swirls on source of diarrhea outbreak, but it’s still Taylor Farms
author: Beth Mole
body: Federal officials on Monday reaffirmed that iceberg lettuce from Taylor Farms still appears to be the source of Cyclospora, the microscopic parasite behind a multi-state surge in explosive diarrhea cases. The assertion was intended to clear up confusion that began swirling over the weekend around a false positive test from the Food and Drug Administration —which Taylor Farms celebrated , sparking confusion. Still, many details of the outbreak remain murky, largely due to the vague recall information Taylor Farms has publicly provided. On Friday, the FDA, along with the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, announced that the FDA's traceback investigations for the outbreak converged on Taylor Farms. Traceback investigations work by tracking backward from sick people and the foods they ate to where those foods came from through the supply chain, down to individual farms or production facilities. When the FDA said their investigation converged on Taylor Farms, the agency means that, over and over again, tracebacks led them to shredded iceberg lettuce grown in Central Mexico and supplied by Taylor Farms—which also goes by Taylor Fresh Foods. body: Nintendo urged a court to dismiss a lawsuit demanding that it pass tariff refunds on to customers, saying that people who bought a Switch console or other products have no legal right to refunds that Nintendo is due to receive from the government. Nintendo said in a motion to dismiss yesterday that plaintiffs are "ask[ing] this Court to invent a legal duty out of whole cloth to retroactively re-price completed sales simply because the legal landscape has changed." Nintendo said the plaintiffs "have no legal entitlement to the tariff refunds Nintendo stands to receive." The lawsuit was filed in April by two customers who seek to represent a class of all US residents who bought Nintendo products from February 2025 to February 2026. California resident Gregory Hoffert and Washington resident Prashant Sharan filed the class action complaint in US District Court for the Western District of Washington, alleging unjust enrichment and a violation of the Washington Consumer Protection Act's prohibition on unfair or deceptive acts. title: Let Tom Hiddleston be your guide to Pompeii's final day
author: Jennifer Ouellette
body: When Mount Vesuvius erupted in 79 CE, it spewed molten rock, pumice, and hot ash over the cities of Pompeii and Herculaneum , killing thousands of people. It's one of the most famous natural disasters in human history, so naturally there have been countless documentaries about Pompeii (and at least one popular song ). But only one features actor Tom Hiddleston taking on the role of a "time detective" to bring the people of Pompeii to vivid life. That would be National Geographic's new three-part docuseries, Pompeii: Out of Time . The Marvel connection made Out of Time happen. Executive producer Kevin Wright was also a producer on the Disney+ series Loki , which included a scene where Hiddleston's Loki travels through time to visit Pompeii. Hiddleston also studied classics at Cambridge University and had visited the Pompeii archaeological site as a teenager, which the Marvel star attributes to indirectly influencing his career path into acting. "There is just a passion to the way Tom tells stories that's infectious," Wright told Ars. "We knew that if we could capture that and put it into [the series], it might interest people who wouldn't normally want to watch a documentary about Pompeii. " Read full article Comments

title: Love Island stars cant accept every post-villa offer. Heres why.
body: Leaving Love Island USA can give a contestant the kind of audience most aspiring creators spend years trying to build. By the time Islanders get their phones back, they may have millions of new followers, brands filling their inboxes, and television producers considering where they could appear next. But leaving the villa does not necessarily mean they are free to accept every opportunity waiting for them. That could matter for Mackenzie "Kenzie" Annis, whose commitment to practicing her splits throughout Season 8 inspired fans to campaign for her to join Dancing With the Stars . Professional dancer Ezra Sosa encouraged the idea beneath Annis' first post-villa TikTok. "The mirrorball is CALLING your name," Sosa commented. "SHUT THE FRONT DOOR," Annis replied. Turning that exchange into an actual trip to the ballroom may require more than an invitation from ABC. According to The Hollywood Reporter , Love Island USA contestants enter a one-year exclusivity period that can limit their television appearances, brand partnerships, and other commercial work after the show. Because Dancing With the Stars airs on ABC and Disney+ , outside Peacock s NBCUniversal family, Annis may need approval before she can compete. Her situation provides a useful look at what happens after Islanders leave Fiji, when sudden internet fame collides with the contracts they signed before anyone knew their names. What happens after Islanders leave the villa? Love Island USA places a group of singles in a Fiji villa, where they date, compete in challenges, and try to remain coupled up long enough to win.

title: Naked mole-rat queens use a chemical signal to suppress fertility in rivals
author: Jacek Krywko
body: Naked mole rats are weird. They spend their lives almost entirely underground, are largely insensitive to some kinds of pain, can persist without oxygen for a long time, rarely develop cancer, and can live past 30 years, which is decades longer than similarly sized rodents. They are also eusocial, like ants, bees, or termites—in a colony that can hold more than a hundred animals, only one female, the queen, breeds. We didn’t know how exactly naked mole-rat queens stop other females from breeding. For a long time, our leading hypothesis was bullying and violence, but that may be a bit impractical, given their kingdoms are vast networks of tunnels that can stretch for up to three kilometers. But now a team of Lewin Lab scientists at Max Delbrück Center for Molecular Medicine in Berlin discovered it’s actually just one part of a sophisticated olfactory signaling program. Running on smell "It's very clear that mole rats actually have quite big noses and smell a lot," says Gary Lewin, a neurobiologist at the Max Delbrück Center for Molecular Medicine and senior author of the study. Naked mole rats have around 1200 olfactory receptor genes, more than mice (which have roughly 1,000 of them) and way more than the few hundred in humans. They're also blind. "They have to be able to navigate and move around without vision, so smell is one of those things that’s enhanced," Lewin said. Read full article Comments

title: X finally ships its rebuilt Android app, but dont expect big changes
body: X has officially rolled out a rebuilt version of its Android app , following through on a project the company first teased roughly a year ago. This Tweet is currently unavailable. It might be loading or has been removed. In an August 2025 post on X , Head of Product Nikita Bier announced he was recruiting what he called the "Android Dream Team," describing the effort in characteristically hyperbolic terms and asking engineers to help "rebuild the X Android app" from the ground up. The recruitment push came as users had periodically flagged bugs and performance issues with the app in the months since; for instance, tapping tweet links from other apps , like WhatsApp, would incorrectly load the main timeline instead of the intended post. Nearly a year after Bier's initial call for engineers, Bier announced on July 20 that the rebuild was complete, calling it one of the largest engineering projects in the company's history. He described the new app as faster, smoother, and more reliable, and said the updated foundation would let the team ship new features more quickly going forward. An X spokesperson elaborated on the changes in an email statement to Mashable: "X modernized the foundation so everything is faster, smoother, and more reliable, including scrolling, loading, and notifications." Beyond the underlying performance improvements, the update doesn't appear to introduce new user-facing features at launch. The company is framing this release as an infrastructure overhaul, with the spokesperson reiterating that Bier's posts on X, including support for hosting Spaces and be

body: Google has introduced two additions to its Gemini "Flash" lineup , along with a specialized cybersecurity model, 3.5 Flash Cyber, while holding off on releasing a long-awaited Gemini 3.5 Pro . In a July 21 blog post , Google announced Gemini 3.6 Flash and Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite, describing them as built to give developers the "efficiency, low latency, and reliability" needed to run AI agents at scale. The post also detailed Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber, a specialized model paired with Google's CodeMender security agent. Our big Guessing Game is back! Enter now for a chance to win an Apple Watch . The announcement also included a brief update on Gemini 3.5 Pro , which Google says "is currently testing with partners," with general availability "as soon as it's ready." The post added that Google has already begun training runs for a future Gemini 4 model. At the Google I/O event in May, Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai announced that Gemini 3.5 Pro would launch in June . Since then, Anthropic and OpenAI have launched new frontier models, Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol , while the Chinese AI lab Moonshot recently launched Kimi K3, a more affordable but still competitive open-source AI model. It's against this backdrop that Google announced its new trio of Gemini models. SEE ALSO: Where is Gemini 3.5 Pro? The AI model announced at Google I/O is still MIA. According to the post, Gemini 3.6 Flash is positioned as the company's general-purpose "workhorse" model, offering improvements in coding, knowledge work, and multimodal tasks compared to 3.5 Flash. Google also priced the model lower than 3.5 Flas

title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement approved; only 350 authors opted out
author: Ashley Belanger
body: On Monday, a judge approved a $1.5 billion settlement between Anthropic and authors, ending the largest copyright class-action ever certified and granting the largest copyright settlement ever reached . Back in May, some authors fought to block the settlement , which was proposed after the court ruled that Anthropic training AI on books was fair use; however, its piracy of works was likely not. Authors opposing the settlement argued that lawyers’ fees were too high and authors’ payouts were too low. Hoping to avoid accepting the estimated $3,000-per-work payout and file separate lawsuits to seek higher damages, a handful of authors tried to opt out past the deadline. Read full article Comments

title: Sales of Valve's Steam Deck crater after recent price hike
author: Kyle Orland
body: Since its 2022 launch, the Steam Deck has consistently been at or near the top of the company's "top selling products by revenue" charts , with brief dips outside of the top 10 treated as newsworthy events . But sales of the popular handheld gaming PC have apparently been on a steep decline since the system relaunched at a higher price in May . Linux-focused gaming site Boiling Steam breaks down the historical data , showing how the Deck's chart position has fallen from fifth place immediately after orders resumed in late May down to 14th place for two weeks in early July (the hardware sits at 12th place in the current edition of those charts ). That's in sharp contrast to 2025, when the Steam Deck never dropped below seventh place on the bestseller charts and was only rarely outside the Top 5. Then Valve warned of "intermittent shortages" for the Steam Deck starting in February, leading to weeks of lower chart positions before the Deck became completely unavailable until May. Read full article Comments

title: Google announces Gemini 3.6 Flash and cybersecurity AI, teases 3.5 Pro and Gemini 4
author: Ryan Whitwam
body: Google announced a significant evolution of its AI models at I/O in May with the release of Gemini 3.5 Flash, and it's not slowing down. The company revealed three new AI models today , including its first version of Gemini geared toward cybersecurity. However, none of the new models is the delayed Gemini 3.5 Pro, which was supposed to launch in June. Gemini 3.5 Flash, which was the star of the show at I/O , has already been deprecated. In its place, developers and users will find Gemini 3.6 Flash. Google makes the usual claims about this model—it's marginally more capable and better at coding, and it has great multimodal features. Google says the changes to 3.6 Flash were made in response to user feedback on the 3.5 release. In general, Gemini 3.5 Flash didn't appear to live up to Google's promises around code generation. Perhaps that is simply a consequence of Google's intense focus on efficiency as businesses have started to fret over the cost of AI tokens. Read full article Comments

title: Pornhub traffic tanked in Spain and Argentina during World Cup final
body: Spain has triumphed as the winner of the 2026 FIFA World Cup , but not before an intense final and the first-ever World Cup halftime show . And, as usual, Pornhub is a barometer for what captured people's attention so much that they clicked away from explicit content. Many Spaniards and Argentines X'ed out of their Pornhub tabs in droves to focus on the game. At kickoff, Pornhub traffic was down 63 percent in Spain and a whopping 77 percent in Argentina, compared to an average Sunday on the tube site. By 5 p.m. ET, around two hours into the game, traffic was down 77 percent in Spain and 74 percent in Argentina (those missing three percent of Argentines had a feeling the final was over prematurely, apparently). Credit: Pornhub There were traffic dips worldwide, but nothing like those in Spain and Argentina. At kickoff, worldwide traffic was down 23 percent compared to an average Sunday, and 29 percent two hours into the game. In the U.S., people seemed to care even less about the gooooooal , with traffic just down 16 percent at kickoff and 21 percent at 5 p.m. ET. Credit: Pornhub Unlike Bad Bunny's Super Bowl halftime show , which got people off the tube site, the World Cup halftime show didn't seem to cause a similar shift. Worldwide traffic was around 20 percent below average at the start, and it nearly returned to typical viewership during the show. Guess Ted Lasso wasn't enticing enough. Credit: Pornhub In the hours after the game ended, Pornhub traffic shot up way above average in Spain (+36 percent at around 9 p.m.), Argentina (+17 percent), and worldwide (+9 percent).

title: The Dink review: Apples pickleball movie is a laugh-out-loud crowd-pleaser
body: With the rise in popularity of pickleball, a "pickleball movie" was inevitable. But I'm frankly relieved that The Dink is in the hands of director Josh Greenbaum. He's the helmer of the brilliant buddy comedy Barb and Star Go to Vista del Mar and the buddy documentary Will Harper . Not only are both of these movies top-tier when it comes to feel-good films, but each centers on older women without making them the butt of any jokes about aging. Instead, both celebrate their heroines for their brightness, humor, horniness, and brave honesty. And The Dink follows in their footsteps, while being a bit more mainstream. The Dink brings together Jake Johnson, Mary Steenburgen, Ed Harris, Patton Oswalt, and Aaron Chen for a buddy comedy that's laugh-out-loud hilarious and terrifically heartwarming. But of course, it begins with the fiery rivalry between tennis players and pickleballers. The Dink is a sensationally silly sports comedy. Jake Johnson stars as lovable loser Dusty Boyd, who'll tell anyone who'll listen about his brush with long-passed glory. As a child, Dusty was a tennis prodigy, coached by his father Chuck (a comically mirthless Ed Harris) to be the next hard-hitting legend. But after a disastrous match against Andy Roddick (yes, Andy Roddick), his reputation was destroyed. So here he is, a lollygagging tennis coach to children at a club run by his father. But Dusty has a chance to redeem himself in his father's eyes when a new enemy invades their pristine tennis courts. You guessed it: pickleballers. In a deranged slideshow presentation, Chuck explains to Dusty how th

title: TreeSize won't renew perpetual-license support unless users subscribe

Further frustration has come from JAM Software's long-standing policy of not providing license keys or installers to TreeSize perpetual license holders after that support period ends. Since 2025, JAM Software has been transitioning most TreeSize editions to subscription models. Today, it sells perpetual licenses only for personal use, which include 12 months of updates, support, and “downloads of older versions, and your license,” plus the option to extend the support period. TreeSize currently has "no plans to discontinue the sale of perpetual licenses for TreeSize Personal," product manager Hendrik Christ told Ars Technica. As perpetual-license maintenance periods expire, customers are discovering that extending support now generally requires subscribing to software they already own the right to use. title: The Light Flip is a minimalist flip phone with a point to prove
body: It’s a Light Phone. It’s a flip phone. | Photo: Light A few years ago, the team at Light shipped the most smartphone-like device they'd ever created. The Light Phone III still kept to its minimalist, no-distractions ethos, but it was a phone-size rectangle with a phone-size touchscreen, a camera, and a number of other features. Some people loved having an almost-smartphone, but Kaiwei Tang, Light's cofounder, says he started getting feedback he didn't expect. Lots of users, particularly young ones, didn't want a smartphone. More than that, actually: They didn't even want something that looked like a smartphone. "We have users who tell us, 'Hey, I hate smartphones. I don't want to be seen using … Read the full story at The Verge.

In a blog post on Tuesday , Google describes Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber as a "cost-efficient and highly capable alternative" to larger, more expensive AI systems, such as the one offered by Anthropic's Mythos. The cybersecurity model is built upon Gemini 3.5 Flash and will be available first to governments and trusted partners via CodeMender, Google's security-focused coding agent . As noted by Google, CodeMender can call upon 3.5 Flash Cyber "multiple times at high speed and low cost," allowing the AI agents to … Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Light Flip is a minimalistic, cheap flip phone that wont distract you
body: Remember Light , the New York-based company that builds super-simple , distraction-free, cheap smartphones ? It's back with a new device; its first-ever flip phone called the Light Flip. SEE ALSO: Galaxy Z Flip 8 rumors: Release date, colors, specs, and more Once again, this is a cheap phone designed to free you from endless scrolling and social media addiction , while still offering the basic tools you need for everyday life. To do this, the company has built an Android-based, minimalistic operating system called LightOS that's mostly textual and displays everything in just two colors. You do get access to music, messages, alarms, and other basic apps, but you don't get to scroll the flashy new TIkTok video; that's what your other phone is for. The Light Flip is available in six colors: Black, Navy, Red, Pink, Yellow, and Light Gray. Credit: Light Phone The Light Flip doesn't stray far from the formula, but approaches it with the body of a flip phone. In many ways, it's far more simple than the company's previous phone, the Light Phone III, which has an OLED touchscreen. The Light Flip is basically your flip phone of old, with a plastic body, a numeric, T9 keypad, some function buttons, and a 2.8-inch, non-touch display. Numeric keypad? That's right, but at least it supports T9 predictive input. Credit: Light Phone Inside, it's powered by a MediaTek MT8873 chip, 6GB of RAM, 128GB of storage, and has a 50-megapixel camera and a (replaceable) 1,800mAh battery. Other amenities include a headphone jack (nice!), a USB-C port, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and 5G connectivity. The camera on

title: Garmin just launched the screen-free Cirqa Smart Band, and it looks a lot like Whoop
body: Garmin just debuted its latest fitness tracker , the Cirqa Smart Band, and it looks a lot like a Whoop . Opens in a new window Credit: Garmin Garmin Cirqa Smart Band $199.99 Learn More When the Oura Ring launched, it unleashed a barrage of competitors coming out with their own smart rings . Now, Whoop is getting that same treatment. First, there was the Fitbit Air , and now, Garmin's Cirqa Smart Band is the latest screen-free health tracker to hit the market. The band is designed for round-the-clock wear, able to do so with 10 days of battery life. Data collected populates in the Garmin Connect app, where users access all their biometrics, including heart rate, Body Battery energy monitoring, Pulse Ox, stress, and skin temperature. Similar to Whoop and Oura, it provides insights into women's health and cycles, compatible with the Natural Cycles app. Even without a screen, the Cirqa detects and records activity. The fabric band is designed for all-day comfort, coming in citron gray, mauve, French gray, dark olive, captain blue, French blue, and black. Plus, it's designed to be worn around the wrist or as an arm band. It comes in two sizes, small/medium and large/extra-large. But unlike the OG screen-free tracker, Whoop, the Cirqa is subscription-free. That means all you have to do is buy the band to gain access to the full suite of features. The Circa Smart Band will be available to order on July 24 for $199.99.

title: New Samsung Galaxy Card launches days before Unpacked event
body: Not content with launching several foldables this week, Samsung is also releasing a new Galaxy credit card. The leaks missed this one, somehow. In a press release on its website, Samsung announced the Galaxy Card, a new credit card on the Visa network. Starting Wednesday, the same day as the Galaxy Unpacked event in London , shoppers who apply for the card online or at any Samsung retail location in the U.S. can get $200 in cash rewards after spending $2,000 in the first 90 days of account opening. In addition, Samsung says cardholders will "earn cash rewards on everyday purchases with increased cash rewards for purchases made from Samsung including the latest devices." The press release includes a picture of the new card alongside a flip phone, though it's unclear if the pictured device is last year's Z Flip 7 or this year's rumored Z Flip 8 . Credit: Samsung SEE ALSO: Everything we expect from Samsung s July event (Fold 8, watches, and glasses!) According to Samsung, additional perks of the Galaxy Card include: 20 percent off Samsung VIP Advantage 5 percent cash rewards on direct Samsung purchases 3 percent rewards on Samsung Wallet purchases 2 percent rewards on streaming services 1 percent reward on all other purchases So, yeah, if you need another credit card and you really like Samsung products, this is certainly an option. It's timed well with the Galaxy Unpacked stream that's set to happen on Wednesday. At Unpacked, Samsung is expected to announce at least three foldable devices and two smartwatches, so you could easily hit that "spend $2,000 within 90 days" number

title: DoorDash is offering DashPass members $40 off Sephora orders over $100 — here’s how to claim on July 22
body: TL;DR: DashPass members can save $40 on Sephora orders of $100+ with the DoorDash promo code BEAUTY40 on July 22. The beauty deal is only available for one day, so you ll want to shop before the redemption limit runs out. DoorDash is ending its Summer of DashPass promotion with a beauty deal for members. On July 22, DashPass subscribers can get $40 off Sephora orders of $100 or more placed through DoorDash with the promo code BEAUTY40. The one-day offer starts at 9 a.m. EDT on July 22, 2026, and is available until the redemption limit of 162,500 uses is reached. To claim the discount, DashPass members must place a qualifying Sephora order on DoorDash with a minimum total of $100. The $40 discount applies only to the order subtotal; fees, taxes, and gratuity still apply. The deal is limited to one redemption per person, isn t available for pickup orders or alcohol purchases, and requires an active DashPass membership . If you ve been waiting for an excuse to refresh your beauty stash, this one-day DashPass deal could be the perfect time to check out your Sephora wishlist.

title: Stellantis taps Mobileye for hands-free driving assist
body: Stellantis will use technology from Intel's Mobileye to power its Level 2 hands-free driving features for its vehicle brands, the companies announced Tuesday. Starting in 2027, Stellantis will begin using Mobileye's EyeQ system-on-a-chip and a data crowdsourcing program called the Road Experience Management , or REM, which uses real-time data from Mobileye-equipped vehicles to build out a global 3D map. The technology will enable "hands-free, eyes-on driving and intelligent lane keeping on unmarked roads in designated areas and conditions," Mobileye said. title: Other Mommy trailer: Yes, the one traumatizing moviegoers ahead of The Odyssey
body: Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey has some truly spooky moments. (Team Circe.) But fans who took to theaters this opening weekend were shook by a surprising trailer ahead of the big show. Meet Other Mommy. If you screamed at that jump scare, you're not alone. Jessica Chastain, who's previously starred in such horror gems as Momma and IT: Chapter Two , returns to the genre with a twisted double-turn. Based on the New York Times bestselling novel Incidents Around the House by Josh Malerman, Other Mommy centers on a little girl named Bela (Arabella Olivia Clark), who's formed a disturbing bond with an entity that looks like her mother. Chastain plays both roles, while Jay Duplass lends support as Daddo. Beyond this promising trailer and an enticing adult cast, Other Mommy also boasts intriguing talent behind the scenes. Horror icon James Wan produces for Blumhouse Atomic Monster, while director Rob Savage ( Host , Dashcam , The Boogeyman ) takes the helm. From the looks of this trailer, he's primed another scream-rich stunner sure to thrill audiences beyond this impactful trailer. Other Mommy opens in theaters Oct. 9.

body: Xteink makes its pocket-sized e-reader even better with a touchscreen and front light.

title: A touchscreen and light make the new X4 Pro the best version of Xteink’s tiny e-readers
body: If you’ve been undecided on which Xteink device to get, the new X4 Pro is it. The familiar story with Xteink’s tiny e-readers plays out once again with its new X4 Pro: upgraded hardware held back by frustrating software. The company’s latest tiny e-reader adds a touchscreen and lighting, so choosing it over a Kindle or a Kobo feels like less of a compromise — until you use it. The new touch controls and some welcome software updates do manage to make the X4 Pro easier to use, but frustrations still abound. There’s a happier ending to the story this time, however, as the team behind the excellent CrossPoint Reader software plan to have their alternative firmware ready for the new device at its launch. If you’ve been considering one of Xteink’s e-readers, the X4 Pro is the one to get, even if it’s the most expensive yet. The X4 Pro is available for purchase today through Xteink’s online store for $99, making it slightly more expensive than the company’s $79 X3 and $69 X4 models . That also brings the X4 Pro closer in price to the $110 entry-level Kindle featuring Amazon’s polished e-reader experience and on-device access to a well-stocked digital bookstore. The X4 Pro lacks both of those things, but with a 4.26-inch, 800 x 480 E Ink screen, it’s another extremely pocketable device from Xteink that’s slightly smaller and thinner than the company’s first e-reader, and considerably smaller than Amazon’s 6-inch Kindle. That thin profile means you’ll need to be prepared for a big compromise: There’s no longer an actual USB-C port. You can still benefit from USB-C functionality but

title: Xteink is back with a new generation of its tiny X4 e-reader — but it wont be for everyone
body: Xteink is back with the second generation of its X4 e-reader. The X4 Pro is here and redesigned, but are there improvements? Xteink's tiny e-readers, which are bigger than a credit card but smaller than a passport, get plenty of attention for their small size, which supposedly can fit on the back of a phone. The X4 has previously made our list of the best e-readers as the best mini e-reader, so we had to try out the second generation ourselves About the Xteink X4 Pro A little bigger than a credit card, the Xteink X4 Pro is perfectly pocket-sized. Credit: Samantha Mangino / Mashable The Xteink X4 Pro, the second generation of the X4, comes with a 4.26-inch display and 219 ppi resolution (Kindle and Kobo both have 300 ppi). It has 8MB RAM and 16MB of storage with up to 256GB of expandable space with a microSD card. It has a 10+ hour battery life, which is about one to two weeks of casual reading, and has a touchscreen with physical buttons on the side. Plus, there's a circular home button, reminiscent of the old iPhone button. The touchscreen is new to the X4, which also has a frontlight. It has a magnetic back, which is compatible with MagSafe, so it can snap to the back of an iPhone. Rather than a USB-C port, it also comes with a magnetic charging port, which uses USB-C. The Xteink X4 Pro charges through a magnetic pogo pin. Credit: Samantha Mangino / Mashable It connects to WiFi, and books are imported to the e-reader via the Xteink app, USB-C, or microSD card. It only supports DRM-free books in text-only PDF and MOBI format, in addition to JPG and BMP image files. The new

title: Spain’s World Cup champions celebrated with custom Oakley Meta glasses
body: TL;DR: Spain s World Cup winners celebrated their victory wearing a one-of-one pair of custom Oakley Meta glasses created exclusively for the team. While fans can t buy that exact edition, Oakley s AI glasses show how wearable tech could help athletes capture plays, review performance, and train smarter. Opens in a new window Credit: Meta Meta Oakley Vanguard Smart AI Glasses $499 at Amazon Shop Now Spain s World Cup celebration came with a special piece of tech. After winning the championship, the Spanish team celebrated wearing custom Oakley Meta glasses made just for the players. The one-of-one edition features custom packaging and lenses etched with the FIFA World Cup Champions mark. While fans can t buy this exact pair, you can get the same AI-powered experience with the Oakley Meta glasses . Credit: FIFA Oakley Meta Vanguard is designed around the needs of athletes. The glasses can capture hands-free footage, letting players record practices, game highlights, or specific plays simply by saying, Hey Meta, take a video. The built-in 12MP ultra-wide camera records in 3K, while stabilization helps keep footage smoother during quick movements like sprints, cuts, and tackles. The glasses can also help players review their performance after the game. When connected to apps like Strava or compatible devices like Garmin , Oakley Meta Vanguard can overlay performance data like sprint speed, distance, and heart rate on captured content, giving athletes more context around their training sessions. title: When your vehicle outlives its cloud: What happens next?
author: Michael Harley
body: Do you use your vehicle’s app to pre‑cool the cabin on a scorching day, confirm the doors are locked from bed, or have the car automatically call for help when the airbags go off? Savor those connected, cloud‑based conveniences while you can—the clock is already ticking on their obsolescence. In a few short years, you may be driving today’s vehicle without many of the features you currently enjoy. How we got hooked on the cloud "Connected services"—cloud-based and networked features that let a vehicle communicate with external systems to deliver apps, data, and remote functions to drivers and passengers—have been around since the 1990s, when GM introduced OnStar as an option. Those early systems were mostly voice‑centric, but they still depended on centralized back‑end systems. Modern versions, which are fully data-driven and cloud-hosted, use much more sophisticated over-the-air data links to allow a considerably better feature set. Read full article Comments

body: Games look gorgeous on its 7.9-inch screen. | Photo: Brandt Ranj / The Verge Woot is celebrating its 22nd anniversary by rolling out a full week of sales, and one of them stands out for gamers looking to get a deal on Nintendo’s latest console. Normally $449.99, the Nintendo Switch 2 is $22 off for Woot customers who already have an account, or $50 off for new customers, when you use coupon code GIFT26 at checkout. Nintendo Switch 2 Where to Buy: $449.99 $428.99 at Woot (with code GIFT26) While a $22 discount (or even a $50 drop) may not seem that significant, discounts have been rare on the console aren’t easy to come by. title: Apps targeted at US troops contain Chinese and Russian code
author: Dell Cameron, wired.com
body: A recent examination of hundreds of mobile apps marketed toward US military personnel found more than one in eight contained software built by companies in China , Russia , or other foreign nations, raising fresh concerns that adversary governments could harvest data revealing where service members live, work, and deploy. According to researchers at Purdue University, the US Military Academy at West Point, and Florida International University , one popular app used by service members to rate living conditions on their own bases include code from Huawei, the Chinese telecom that US regulators flagged as a national security threat in 2020. Two others were built by Russian companies and incorporate the Russian ad service Yandex. The largely unregulated advertising industry that tracks Americans online treats civilians and service members mostly the same—unless there is profit in telling them apart —despite evidence that exposure can reveal troop deployments, unit movements, and the routines of personnel within intelligence facilities and hardened shelters where nuclear weapons are believed to be stored. title: 2027 Kia Seltos first drive gives you plenty of SUV for not much $
author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
body: Kia provided flights from Washington, DC, to Orange County, California, and accommodation so Ars could drive the Seltos. Ars does not accept paid editorial content. NEWPORT BEACH, Calif.—Although it sometimes feels like automakers have all but abandoned everything other than high-end luxury model vehicles, it's not universally true. While some car companies have lobbied the government, terrified, asking to build protective walls to fend off cheap Chinese competition, others have looked at the problem from a different angle, trying to meet the moment. One such effort is the new Kia Seltos, a small SUV that takes inspiration from the Telluride but with a starting price of just $24,990 plus delivery charge. The US almost didn't get the Seltos in the first place; the first-generation model was designed with China in mind. Seltos take two is more considered, and also a little more substantial. The new body with its Telluride-like design cues has been stretched, mostly in the wheelbase. That said, at 174.4 inches (4,430 mm) long, 72 inches (1,829 mm) wide, and 64.9 inches (1,648 mm) tall, it's still a subcompact, one segment smaller than something like a Toyota RAV4 or Mazda CX-5 . Kia is offering a choice of three different powertrain variants. The 1.6 L hybrid is the one we're most interested in. But that version won't be in showrooms until Q4 this year; don't expect an Ars first drive of the hybrid Seltos until early next year. title: Nintendo keeps making shooters more friendly and approachable
body: Nintendo does a lot of weird things , but Splatoon might just be one of the strangest - or at least the boldest. The series started as an attempt to muscle into the ultra-competitive online shooter space, but in a way that feels a world away from the violence and bombast of Call of Duty or Battlefield . Splatoon is essentially candy-coated paintball starring a race of skate punk squid creatures , and across three entries Nintendo has just about perfected the formula. The series introduced the idea of competitive shooters to whole new audiences, and now it's expanding: Splatoon Raiders takes it in a different direction, but with the same focus o … Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Halliday’s latest smart glasses feature a much-improved display
body: I was not a fan of the original Halliday glasses. The Gen 2 are better. | Image: Halliday I first slipped on Halliday's original smart glasses at CES 2025. I was not a fan. The glasses had a tiny, movable display window embedded into the frame that was incredibly finicky to see, and my 30-minute demo left me with achy eyeballs. It was an interesting concept with terrible execution, especially compared to the several other smart glasses on the show floor . So I was skeptical when a few weeks ago, Halliday reached out to say its second attempt at smart glasses was much better. Well, having tried the Halliday Gen 2, I'm happy to report these are better glasses. That secret display window is gone, replaced by more traditional but dis … Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Splatoon Raiders is a spiffy single-player makeover for Nintendos coolest series
body: I love Splatoon . I have loved Splatoon since all the way back to its Wii U debut in 2015. But Splatoon doesn't always love me back. Every time I boot up Splatoon 3 , I rediscover I'm not ready for the series' signature competitive multiplayer arenas. Everyone who still plays Splatoon this long after launch is really, really good too good for me to have much of a good time. The smattering of solo and co-op experiences that exist on the periphery can't hold my attention for very long. It is delightful, then, to report that Splatoon Raiders is pretty damn good. The first single-player-focused entry in the decade-old series is one of the most purely fun (on a moment-to-moment basis) games on the Nintendo Switch 2 . Splatoon Raiders synthesizes some of the best side modes in previous Splatoon games into something that grabbed me and didn't let go for several days, thanks in part to some non-competitive co-op features. I can't wait to play more once it goes on sale. SEE ALSO: Nintendo's potentially last Switch 1 game is absolutely worth your time Splatoon Raiders melds every non-competitive Splatoon mode into one game I say this not as criticism, but as a compliment: Splatoon Raiders feels like DLC for a mainline Splatoon game that grew out of control. This is still a third-person shooter where you, as a squid kid, use a variety of weapons to spread ink of your own color across a variety of battlefields. You don't run very fast, but if your ink color is on the ground, you can quickly swim around in it, recovering ammo and health in the process. Everything about the basic mechani

title: Which countries have banned social media for kids?
body: Social media bans for kids are quickly becoming the global legislation trend of the moment. Children under 16 are finding themselves at the center of political conversations, with countries including Australia , the UK , Canada , Brazil , Austria , and Indonesia , and more attempting to wrangle Big Tech companies' hold on young people's attention. SEE ALSO: Australia's kid social media ban is ineffective, study suggests Online age verification laws are taking shape by the month, with governments citing the need to address issues including child sexual exploitation , cyberbullying , self-harm , and the impact on kids' mental health , as well as "addictive" design, violent content , and porn . However, these laws have also come under severe criticism for efficiency and their impact on digital privacy and free speech . Plus, kids are already finding ways around them. So, where are we at, globally? Which countries have followed Australia's example and are rolling out their own social media bans? Here's the list, alphabetically, and incidentally starting with the one who started it all. Australia The first to do it, Australia set a global precedent in Dec. 2025 with its world-first social media ban for children under 16 . The Online Safety Amendment (Social Media Minimum Age) Act requires social media platforms such as TikTok, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, X, and Snapchat to implement age verification and take "reasonable steps" to prevent kids under 16 from creating accounts. However, a July 2026 study by Australian government advisors found the ban so far ineffective . Plus, R

For the money, you re getting a 32-inch Fast IPS display with QHD 2560x1440 resolution which is shipped and sold by Amazon, getting to you as early as the next day if you re a Prime member (at the time of writing). As an upgrade over older standard monitors, it offers 1.7 times the pixel density of Full HD, giving games noticeably cleaner edges and finer environmental detail without demanding as much from your PC as a 4K panel. The 180Hz refresh rate and 1ms GtG response time are the draws for anyone regularly playing quick multiplayer games. Whether you re sprinting through Fortnite , tracking fast-moving enemies in Overwatch , or lining up a last-second shot in Call of Duty , the higher refresh rate should keep movement looking much smoother than it would on a typical 60Hz or 75Hz model. title: The Lego Icons French Café has dropped to its best-ever price at Amazon — save over $15
body: SAVE OVER $10: As of July 21, the Lego Icons French Caf has hit a new low price of $63.74 at Amazon. That's $16.25 off its full price of $79.99. Opens in a new window Credit: Lego Lego Icons French Caf Paris Building Kit (10362) $63.74 at Amazon $79.99 Save $16.25 Get Deal Lego sets can be quite costly, but Amazon is a helpful place to check out for great deals. Even today, the retailer has some nice surprises in store. As of July 21, the charming Lego Icons French Caf set has dropped to a new low price of $63.74 at Amazon. Compared to this Lego set's full price of $79.99, this deal saves you $16.25. At the moment, it has a "limited time deal" tag, so the clock is ticking to pick it up while it's still on sale. SEE ALSO: Lego is releasing the biggest Art Masterpiece ever: Everything you need to know about Gustav Klimt The Kiss In total, it comes with 1,101 pieces that create a classic French bistro, complete with hanging flowerpots, ornate seating, and accessories such as food and crockery to bring the scene together. The double doors at the front also open to reveal a small interior with a seating area, so you can choose whether to keep them open or closed once you're all finished. It's a set recommended for adults, likely due to its higher piece count, more detailed assembly, and the fact that it's meant for display rather than play. However, younger builders can still get the fun of piecing it together if they want to; they'll just need an older individual around to help out. And now Amazon has made that co-operative box cheaper than we ve ever seen. As of July 21, the Magic: The Gathering Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Team-Up Box is available at Amazon for $29.99 cutting $20 from its $49.99 list price, giving you roughly 40% off the complete multiplayer set. This not only dips well under TCGplayer s market price of $36.80 , but is also the lowest price we ve ever seen the TMNT-themed MTG box on Amazon, as confirmed with price tracker camelcamelcamel . To be clear, this is not another standard booster box designed for opening packs. title: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 21, 2026
body: Today's Wordle answer should be easy to solve if you can keep a secret. If you just want to be told today's word, you can jump to the bottom of this article for today's Wordle solution revealed. But if you'd rather solve it yourself, keep reading for some clues, tips, and strategies to assist you. SEE ALSO: Mahjong, Sudoku, free crossword, and more: Play games on Mashable SEE ALSO: NYT Connections hints today: Clues, answers for July 21, 2026 Where did Wordle come from? Originally created by engineer Josh Wardle as a gift for his partner, Wordle rapidly spread to become an international phenomenon, with thousands of people around the globe playing every day. Alternate Wordle versions created by fans also sprang up, including battle royale Squabble , music identification game Heardle , and variations like Dordle and Quordle that make you guess multiple words at once . Wordle eventually became so popular that it was purchased by the New York Times , and TikTok creators even livestream themselves playing . What's the best Wordle starting word? The best Wordle starting word is the one that speaks to you. But if you prefer to be strategic in your approach, we have a few ideas to help you pick a word that might help you find the solution faster. One tip is to select a word that includes at least two different vowels, plus some common consonants like S, T, R, or N. What happened to the Wordle archive? The entire archive of past Wordle puzzles was originally available for anyone to enjoy whenever they felt like it, but it was later taken down , with the website's creator stating it

title: NYT Strands hints, answers for July 21, 2026
body: Today's NYT Strands hints are easy if you're a good writer. Strands , the New York Times ' elevated word-search game, requires the player to perform a twist on the classic word search. Words can be made from linked letters up, down, left, right, or diagonal, but words can also change direction, resulting in quirky shapes and patterns. Every single letter in the grid will be part of an answer. There's always a theme linking every solution, along with the "spangram," a special, word or phrase that sums up that day's theme, and spans the entire grid horizontally or vertically. SEE ALSO: Mahjong, Sudoku, free crossword, and more: Play games on Mashable By providing an opaque hint and not providing the word list, Strands creates a brain-teasing game that takes a little longer to play than its other games, like Wordle and Connections . If you're feeling stuck or just don't have 10 or more minutes to figure out today's puzzle, we've got all the NYT Strands hints for today's puzzle you need to progress at your preferred pace. SEE ALSO: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 21, 2026 NYT Strands hint for today s theme: Write there The words are related to paper. Today s NYT Strands theme plainly explained These words describe writing utensils. NYT Strands spangram hint: Is it vertical or horizontal? Today's NYT Strands spangram is horizontal. title: macOS 27 Beta has a secret Siri AI interface. How to try it.
body: You've waited long enough for Apple's relaunch of Siri as an AI assistant, and now you can finally try out the Siri AI beta for iOS . But, psst, don't tell anyone, but there's even more Siri AI for you to try out with a secret Siri AI interface on Mac. A Reddit user on the r/MacOSBeta subreddit has discovered a "hidden lightweight UI" for Siri AI in the macOS 27 Golden Gate beta . This feature basically provides users with an array of AI tools, presented in a pop-up menu whenever text is highlighted. Reddit The Siri AI pop-up interface includes AI tools such as Rewrite, Proofread, "How does this sound?," and "Edit with Siri." There are also menu options for summarization and providing bullet points from the selected text. Siri AI can also provide a selection of specific tools dependent on the context of what's selected. For example, highlighting a phone number provides a pop-up to "Add to contacts" or when selecting an address the pop-up offers an "Open in Maps" option. There are tools for creating calendar events, composing messages and emails, and tracking flights and packages as well. Why is the feature hidden? Well, as MacRumors points out, the Siri AI interface in macOS 27 developer beta 3 and public beta 1 is simply an unfinished product. Certain features don't yet work how they're supposed to. Some options don't work at all. Clearly, Apple has yet to make it available because it's simply not ready. The allegation was unusual: Gaffney had used his iPhone to snap photos of nude prisoners he had encountered in his job—and he had then shared those photos with "several female civilian employees inside the Sheriff's Office." The main claim involved a local man suffering a mental health crisis on the morning of January 30, 2026. Five fully uniformed deputies had arrived at the house and were in the upstairs bedroom. Their detainee was naked from the waist down and had just been persuaded to put on some pants. Read full article Comments

title: AI just disproved a major math problem. A mathematician explains.
body: While the rest of the world was watching the World Cup on Sunday evening, mathematician Levent Alp ge casually announced on X that he had used Anthropic's Fable 5 to disprove the Jacobian conjecture. This Tweet is currently unavailable. It might be loading or has been removed. The Jacobian conjecture is a long-standing open problem in algebraic geometry that's bedeviled highly accomplished mathematicians for almost 90 years. It was included in "Smale's problems," a list of unresolved math problems put forward by mathematician Stephen Smale in 1998. But that proved no obstacle for Fable 5, Anthropic's latest frontier AI model. (You may remember Fable 5 as the public version of Claude Mythos Preview , the AI model that Anthropic says has such advanced cybersecurity capabilities that it was too dangerous for public release .) Alp ge is part of Harvard's Society of Fellows, and his Linkedin profile notes an affiliation with Anthropic. We've reached out to Anthropic with questions about Alp ge's work. Meanwhile, for those of us who don't understand polynomial functions from n-dimensional space, the question is: Does this represent a major breakthrough in artificial intelligence, or mathematics, or neither? "This did not cause me to update my priors about what AI can and can't do," professor and mathematician Andrew Blumberg told Mashable. "This is exactly the kind of thing I would expect AI to be able to do. If there was a counterexample that was concise and easy to state that people haven't found because it's a pain to search through all this stuff, AI will find it." Blumberg,

title: Firefighting drones in the works as wildfires plague US nearly year-round
author: Jeremy Hsu
body: Drones capable of spraying water and fire retardants have been practicing how to snuff out wildfires early in California and Alaska this summer—demonstrating a possible rapid-response tool for firefighting as climate change transforms wildfire season into a nearly year-round risk for much of the United States. Most drones are significantly smaller than crewed aircraft and have shorter flight ranges, meaning they cannot replace large airtankers in delivering massive payloads of water or other fire-suppressing payloads to remote wildfires. But companies and fire agencies—along with organizers of the $11 million XPRIZE competition—are testing whether drones can help firefighting crews respond more quickly to small fires and put them out before they become more destructive. The California Department of Forestry and Fire Protection, also known as CAL FIRE, ran its own field test involving five autonomous drones that worked together to deploy between 500 and 1,000 gallons of foam combined for suppressing fires on July 15, according to the TV station KPMH . The demonstration was organized with the help of the nonprofit FireWERX and the California-based company Seneca , which is making the drones commercially available starting in 2026. title: Judge halts Paramount's $111B purchase of Warner Bros. in win for US states
author: Jon Brodkin
body: A federal judge today ordered Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros. Discovery to halt their $111 billion merger, handing an early win to states that sued to block the deal. The ruling granted a temporary restraining order prohibiting the firms from completing the merger and from consolidating their operations. The temporary order is only in effect for 14 days, but it can be converted into a preliminary injunction that would prevent the merger from being completed until the case is resolved. The temporary restraining order can be extended past the 14-day period if more time is needed to rule on a preliminary injunction. A group of 12 states led by California sued the companies last week in an attempt to block the deal, which had been approved by the Trump administration . The states say the merger will eliminate competition by combining two of the five major Hollywood movie studios, and two of the five major owners of basic cable TV channels. title: The Space Force is now seeking to buy up to $30 billion in rocket launches
author: Stephen Clark
body: It seems as though $5.6 billion wasn't enough. That was the news from the US Space Force on Friday, when military officials announced they were tripling the maximum value of one of the service's National Security Space Launch contracts to $17 billion. The expansion of the Space Force's National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 3 contract comes as the Pentagon signals rising demand for military satellite launches. The NSSL program is set up to allow Space Systems Command, which oversees the Space Force's launch program, to select from a pool of launch providers for individual missions to deliver the military's satellites to orbit. The NSSL program has two parts. Lane 1 covers the Space Force's more risk-tolerant missions, such as medium-lift launches with experimental payloads or rideshare missions carrying satellites for the Pentagon's surveillance or data relay constellations . Lane 2 includes higher-priority strategic missions, like the government's largest and most expensive spy satellites, or radiation-hardened communications satellites designed to survive a nuclear war. Read full article Comments

It’s a smattering of the publisher 2K’s biggest games of the 2010s, including BioShock Infinite, Risk of Rain 2, Borderlands 3, and XCOM: Enemy Unknown . The bundle will be available until August 7th, though the bundle page notes that keys for some games are already starting to run low. All of the games work on Windows, while some support MacOS and SteamOS. 2K Megahits 2026 Bundle Where to Buy: $465 $15 at Humble One of my personal favorite games, Risk of Rain 2 , is included in the bundle. In the third-person roguelike, you’ll face off against increasingly difficult waves of enemies, fighting to stay alive as long as you can and collecting power-ups and useful perks before extracting to the next zone to do it all again. It’s just challenging enough to make victory satisfying, and fun to play alone or with friends. You also get 2012’s XCOM: Enemy Unknown , a legendary turn-based strategy game that’s usually $15 on its own, to give you a sense of the bundle’s value. The bundle also notably includes three games from the Borderlands universe. title: The moments that defined the 2026 World Cup
body: Every four years, the World Cup reminds us why we fall in love with sports in the first place. The 2026 tournament was one of firsts. For the first time, three countries Canada, Mexico, and the United States shared hosting duties. For the first time, the field expanded from 32 to 48 teams, giving more nations a chance to compete on the sport's biggest stage and more federations the opportunity to showcase their depth. That expansion brought historic debuts for Cape Verde, Jordan, Cura ao, and Uzbekistan. Their first appearances delivered some of the tournament's most unforgettable moments. It also felt like a farewell tour. Cristiano Ronaldo. Lionel Messi. Luka Modrić. Neymar. Son Heung-min. Guillermo Ochoa. A generation that shaped the sport for two decades took one last bow on the biggest stage, with several likely making their final World Cup appearances. Taken together, those storylines made this World Cup feel bigger than any single match. Over the course of several weeks, the tournament gave us unforgettable goals, shocking upsets, viral celebrations, heartbreaking exits, and moments that transcended soccer altogether. Here are the moments that defined the 2026 World Cup. Vozinha and the Blue Sharks The best feel-good story of the World Cup has to be Cape Verde's historic run and their goalkeeper, Vozinha (n e Josimar Jos vora Dias). The 40-year-old keeper and Cape Verde stalwart rocketed to international fame after a heroic performance against eventual champions Spain, keeping a clean sheet against the European giants to earn the 500,000-strong nation its first-

title: AT&T loses key ruling in bid to stop offering basic phone service in California
author: Jon Brodkin
body: California can keep enforcing rules that require AT T to offer basic phone service to new customers in its wireline territory, following a federal judge's ruling last week. AT T sued California in May in a bid to end the state's Carrier of Last Resort (COLR) rules that require it to offer telephone service to any potential customer in its territory. AT T asked for a preliminary injunction that would prevent California from enforcing the COLR rules while the litigation continues. To win a preliminary injunction, AT T had to show it is likely to succeed on the merits of its claim that California rules are preempted by a Federal Communications Commission order. US District Judge Linda Lopez denied AT T's request for a preliminary injunction during a motion hearing on Thursday, according to a docket entry . The case is in US District Court for the Southern District of California. Read full article Comments

title: RIP bargain bin: The price impact of Sony's disc-free PlayStation plan
author: Kyle Orland
body: Since Sony announced that it was planning to stop producing physical PlayStation discs in 2028 , many people have been justifiably worried about how they will maintain ownership and long-term game access in a disc-free world. But halting production of PlayStation discs also means eliminating the market for cheap used PlayStation discs, which are often available for well below the price of a digital download. How much is this disc-based discount worth? To find out, Ars analyzed how the prices of 19 current top-selling PlayStation games varied between digital downloads and physical discs (both new and used). We found that, while used discs are the cheapest option for most games for most of the year, the frequent and periodic deep discounts offered on the PlayStation Store often undercut the cheapest game disc options. That state of affairs will likely persist even in a future without PlayStation discs. Our prices are so low, you'll think we've suffered brain damage For our analysis, we started with Sony's list of the bestsellers on the PlayStation Store . We eliminated any games that weren't available on a physical disc, then used the database over at PlatPrices.com to determine both the "Standard" price and the lowest "Discount" price offered for those games on the PlayStation Store in the last year (as well as the total number of days the discount price was offered). Read full article Comments

title: Kalshi gains 3 million users amid World Cup gambling
body: Spain has won the 2026 FIFA World Cup and millions of new Kalshi users either won or lost money betting on the game. According to prediction market Kalshi, the platform brought in 3 million new users during the World Cup tournament, which kicked off on June 11 and ended with the Spain vs. Argentina final on July 19. In addition to the millions of new users, the company said that the World Cup broke a platform record for the most money traded in a single market. Kalshi says more than $1.2 billion in trades, the term the company uses to describe bets, were made to predict the winner of the World Cup. Kalshi had a large presence throughout the World Cup. The company partnered with stars of the sport, such as Luka Modrić of the Croatia national team, who served as a brand ambassador and appeared in a commercial. Kalshi also had a partnership with the Argentina national team and received a featured post for the brand on Lionel Messi's Instagram account, which has over 512 million followers. A commercial featuring A-lister Timoth e Chalamet also appeared frequently in the U.S. market. Despite their constant presence throughout the tournament, neither Kalshi nor its largest competitor, Polymarket, were official sponsors of the FIFA World Cup. A much smaller, new prediction market called ADI Predictstreet was deemed the "Official Prediction Market Partner of the FIFA World Cup 2026." However, Kalshi even found a way in there. title: Jimothy the raccoon is taking over the internet
body: A very unusual raccoon from Seattle has become the internet's newest sweetheart. Since appearing in a brief video filmed in the city s Ballard neighborhood, Jimothy has accumulated millions of views and inspired murals, tattoos, merchandise, government public-service announcements, a city proclamation, and even a surprise appearance at a Seattle Mariners game. Online, fans have promoted him to "King Jimothy," "Saint Jimothy," and Seattle s "sweet prince." "Hot Jimothy Summer," a play on Megan Thee Stallion's "hot girl summer," has become the unofficial slogan surrounding the unusually shaped animal. SEE ALSO: The internet is worried about Punch the monkey. The zoo says he's OK. Jimothy s sudden fame began with one fairly reasonable question: What exactly are we looking at? This Tweet is currently unavailable. It might be loading or has been removed. Meet Jimothy, Seattle's unusually shaped raccoon Kiana Hall encountered Jimothy while walking with her husband through Ballard on July 13. Hall initially thought the animal moving beneath a parked car was a cat and pulled out her phone to get a better look. Then it emerged with the familiar masked face of a raccoon and a much less familiar body shape. Hall posted the video on Instagram and named the raccoon Jimothy, a decision based less on a complicated backstory than an immediate feeling. "He just looked like a 'Jimothy' to me," Hall told TODAY.com . "I don't have any other explanation other than I looked at him and he looked like a 'Jimothy.'" The name stuck. The video garnered millions of views, while people around Ballard b

title: LG’s glossy OLED gaming monitor is rare to find under $400
body: LG’s UltraGear Glossy OLED gaming monitor deliver deep, true-to-life blacks. | Image: LG If you’ve been thinking about upgrading your gaming monitor, LG’s 27-inch 27GX704A-B pairs a glossy WOLED panel with a fast refresh rate, and it’s currently on sale for $379.99 (about $70 off) at Amazon and directly from LG , marking a new low price. It originally launched at $799.99, but has been available for under $500 since May. LG UltraGear Glossy OLED gaming monitor (27-inches) Where to Buy: $449 $379.99 at Amazon $449 $379.99 at LG The monitor offers a 1440p resolution and a glossy coating, which isn’t a combination of specs we often see at this price. Most OLED monitors under $400 use QD-OLED panels, which can give blacks a slight bluish tint. LG’s WOLED monitor delivers truer-looking blacks while the glossy coating helps colors appear richer and more vibrant. It doesn’t sacrifice speed, either. The monitor boasts a fast 240Hz refresh rate with variable refresh rate support (Nvidia G-Sync compatible and AMD FreeSync Premium Pro), along with a fast 0.03ms response time to keep games looking sharp in motion. It comes with a solid array of ports for the price, with two HDMI 2.1 ports, DisplayPort 1.4, and USB-A ports, making it easy to connect a PC, consoles, and accessories. Rounding things out, the stand is fully adjustable, too, allowing you to raise, lower, tilt, swivel, or rotate the display into portrait mode for a more comfortable setup. Or, because it has a VESA mount, you can yank it off and toss it onto a monitor arm of your choice.

title: What does The Odyssey translator Emily Wilson think of Christopher Nolans film?
body: We can't ask Homer what he thinks of Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey , but we can ask someone who knows it better than most. Emily Wilson, the British-American classicist whose 2017 translation of the epic poem was one of the director's inspirations , was asked her opinion on The Odyssey film by the Associated Press . "I want to celebrate the fact that so many more people are aware of The Odyssey and were brought back to the poem, she told AP. "But at the same time, I had the sense that Christopher Nolan was trying to pack everything in while missing themes that were essential to the poem." SEE ALSO: 'The Odyssey' hints at 3 myths without detailing them. Here are the untold stories. Nolan has cited Wilson's work as among his influences for the film, notably Homer's opening line, "Tell me about a complicated man." Granted, it's nothing short of a herculean task to try and fit every last element of Homer's 12,000-line poem into a film that runs just under three hours, despite that IMAX screen size . Nolan does hit the major plot points of The Odyssey 's 20-year chronology the Trojan Horse, Penelope's (Anne Hathaway) sleazy suitors, Odysseus (Matt Damon) and his men running into the cyclops Polythemus (Bill Irwin) , the sirens, the Scylla monster, and the nymph Calypso (Charlize Theron) along the way he leaves many myths deliberately untold. Featured Video For You What It Was Like Shooting One of Oppenheimer s Most Remarkable Moments For one thing, Wilson's translation famously reexamined the role of women in The Odyssey , like the characters of Penelope and Circe. And Nolan's

title: F1 in Belgium: Machine learning algorithms are ruining the sport
author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
body: Formula 1 returned to the Spa-Francorchamps circuit for the Belgium Grand Prix this weekend. This ribbon of tarmac in the Ardennes is one of the sport's historic venues, featuring in the first F1 season way back in 1950. But as yesterday's race showed, the current F1 machinery is utterly ill-suited to this dramatic venue. As we saw at Suzuka in Japan earlier this year, a 2026 F1 battery is able to power the race car's electric motor for only a few seconds a lap; for much of the rest of the lap, the energy-starved cars have less power going to their rear wheels than in the junior F2 feeder series. The best race track in the world For many, including much of the current F1 grid, Spa is considered perhaps the planet's greatest race track. In fact, both Spa and the Belgian Grand Prix predate the F1 world championship, with its first race held in 1922. Back then, the circuit was a 9-mile loop of public roads, taking in the towns of Malmedy and Stavelot before returning to Francorchamps. In the postwar years, the layout shrank slightly to 8.7 miles (14.1 km) and was ferociously fast, with average lap speeds of up to 150 mph (240 km/h)—and ferociously dangerous, given cars with huge amounts of power and minimal concessions to safety. title: Dr. Jill Lepore on why AI backlash is vital for the future
body: Today, I’m talking with Harvard professor and New Yorker staff writer Dr. Jill Lepore about her new book, The Rise and Fall of the Artificial State , which comes out on August 25. Jill is one of the best writers there is at identifying institutional patterns in history, and Decoder is a show about systems, so this book could not possibly be more in our zone. You build systems by measuring things — gathering information, quantifying it as data, and taking action on it. And Jill’s big thesis in the book is that this kind of systemic measurement is really a dehumanizing act, one that flattens human concerns into something, well, artificial. These artificial structures touch everything — not just in the more obvious places like in apps or AI, but all the way up to politics and governance itself, and Jill’s insights here are a telling portrait of the way Big Tech and its billionaire figureheads have managed to break the very idea of the state itself. Verge subscribers, don’t forget you get exclusive access to ad-free Decoder wherever you get your podcasts. Head here . Not a subscriber? You can sign up here . You’ll hear Jill tell me she wanted to write a “kind of grumpy book,” but in my reading, it’s a pretty dead-ahead look at the last century and a half of technological and social developments and how we got to a place where bots are maybe running everything. Jill’s idea of the artificial state starts with the very idea of quantification, from the first stabs at taking a census thousands of years ago to the invention of modern political polling in the 1930s. But where sh
